LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT KARY WITT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED AS TO THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR ALTERNATIVELY, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. THE COURT CONCLUDES THAT THE COMPLAINT IS INSUFFICIENT AS A MATTER OF LAW AND ELECTS TO TREAT THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR ALTERNATIVELY, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS A MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. "[C]OURTS HAVE HELD THAT A DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT NECESSARILY INCLUDES A TEST OF THE SUFFICIENCY OF THE COMPLAINT, AND THE MOTION IS TREATED AS A MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS." (PEOPLE EX REL. DEPT. OF TRANSPORTATION V. OUTDOOR MEDIA GROUP (1993) 13 CAL.APP.4TH 1067, 1074.) IN THE COMPLAINT, PLAINTIFF DOES NOT ALLEGE FACTS PERTAINING TO THE CLAIM FILING REQUIREMENT OF THE GOVERNMENT CLAIMS ACT. (SEE COMPLAINT; REPLY, P. 1:13-15.) "IT IS WELL ESTABLISHED THAT THE PLEADINGS DETERMINE THE SCOPE OF RELEVANT ISSUES ON A SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION." (NIETO V. BLUE SHIELD OF CALIFORNIA LIFE & HEALTH INS. CO. (2010) 181 CAL.APP.4TH 60, 74.) THE COURT DOES NOT CONSIDER THE EVIDENCE PROFFERED IN PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION ON THIS SUBJECT. PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED 5 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND, IF HE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, TO ALLEGE COMPLIANCE OR EXCUSE FROM COMPLIANCE FROM THE CLAIM FILING REQUIREMENT OF THE GOVERNMENT CLAIMS ACT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: LESLIE NICHOLS, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =302/LN

ORDER DENYING DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT KARY WITT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - THE COURT DENIES SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. DEFENDANT'S MOTION IS NOT AN IMPROPER RECONSIDERATION PURSUANT TO CCP 437C(F)(2). JUDGE NICHOLS TREATED DEFENDANT'S PRIOR MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AS A MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS AND HE DID NOT ENTERTAIN ANY OF THE PARTIES' EVIDENCE. SEE ORDER FILED AUGUST 14, 2013. THE COURT DENIES SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO ISSUES 1, 2, AND 4. AS TO EACH ISSUE, THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING WHETHER MR. WITT'S CONDUCT WAS THE RESULT OF PERSONAL ANIMUS AGAINST PLAINTIFF AND/OR HIS UNION BECAUSE OF THE NEGATIVE POSTINGS REGARDING MR. WITT THAT WERE ON THE UNION'S WEBSITE. SEE EVIDENCE CITED IN SUPPORT OF PLAINTIFF'S FACTS P8-P10 AND P32-P33 (DECLARATION OF SARAH VARELA IN SUPPORT OF OPPOSITION TO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, 2 (WITT DEPOSITION. PP. 121:23-122:14; 124:1-20; 127:24-128:1; 128:25-129:11; 129:17-25).) DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO THESE PORTIONS OF THE WITT DEPOSITION ARE OVERRULED. (SEE DEFENDANT'S OBJECTION TO PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE, OBJECTIONS 12-15.) THE COURT DENIES SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO ISSUE 3 BECAUSE THAT ISSUE (THE PROPRIETY OF INJUNCTIVE RELIEF) DOES NOT COMPLETELY DISPOSE OF A CAUSE OF ACTION AS REQUIRED BY CCP 437C(F)(1). PLAINTIFF PLEADS ONE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION CIVIL CODE SECTION 51.7 AND REMEDIES UNDER RELATED STATUTES. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

Off Calendar Jan-03-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. SARAH VARELA & ATTY. JOHN J. DAVIS OF DAVIS CARELL & BOWE,
TEL. NO. 597-7200 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JULIE VEIT OF HANSON BRIDGETT, TEL. NO. 777-3200 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT KARY WITT's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication - The Court denies summary judgment and summary adjudication. Defendant's motion is not an improper reconsideration pursuant to CCP 437c(f)(2). Judge Nichols treated Defendant's prior motion for summary judgment as a motion for judgment on the pleadings and he did not entertain any of the parties' evidence. See Order filed August 14, 2013. The Court denies summary adjudication as to issues 1, 2, and 4. As to each issue, there is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether Mr. Witt's conduct was the result of personal animus against plaintiff and/or his union because of the negative postings regarding Mr. Witt that were on the union's website. See evidence cited in support of Plaintiff's Facts P8-P10 and P32-P33 (Declaration of Sarah Varela in Support of Opposition to Motion for Summary Judgment, 2 (Witt Deposition. pp. 121:23-122:14; 124:1-20; 127:24-128:1; 128:25-129:11; 129:17-25).) Defendant's objections to these portions of the Witt Deposition are overruled. (See Defendant's Objection to Plaintiff's Evidence, Objections 12-15.) The Court denies summary adjudication as to issue 3 because that issue (the propriety of injunctive relief) does not completely dispose of a cause of action as required by CCP 437c(f)(1). Plaintiff pleads one cause of action for violation Civil Code section 51.7 and remedies under related statutes. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-14-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. SARAH VARELA & JOHN J. DAVIS JR. OF DAVIS, COWELL & BOWE LLP, TEL. NO. 597-7200 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. KIMON MANOLIUS OF HANSON BRIDGETT, LLP, TEL. NO. 777-3200 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT KARY WITT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is Denied as to the motion for summary judgment, or alternatively, summary adjudication. The Court concludes that the Complaint is insufficient as a matter of law and elects to treat the motion for summary judgment, or alternatively, summary adjudication as a motion for judgment on the pleadings. "[C]ourts have held that a defendant's motion for summary judgment necessarily includes a test of the sufficiency of the complaint, and the motion is treated as a motion for judgment on the pleadings." (People ex rel. Dept. of Transportation v. Outdoor Media Group (1993) 13 Cal.App.4th 1067, 1074.) In the Complaint, Plaintiff does not allege facts pertaining to the claim filing requirement of the Government Claims Act. (See Complaint; Reply, p. 1:13-15.) "It is well established that the pleadings determine the scope of relevant issues on a summary judgment motion." (Nieto v. Blue Shield of California Life & Health Ins. Co. (2010) 181 Cal.App.4th 60, 74.) The Court does not consider the evidence proffered in Plaintiff's opposition on this subject. Plaintiff is granted 5 days leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege compliance or excuse from compliance from the claim filing requirement of the Government Claims Act. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Leslie Nichols, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/LN
